page_intro: We recruit undergraduates, medical students, orthopaedic residents and fellows, and postdoctoral collaborators on a rolling basis. You will own real projects and see them through to publication, working alongside skilled clinicians and engineers.

openings.title: Junior Specialist, Clinical Research
openings.detail: Support clinical and computational research in the COSI Lab: study coordination, data analysis, and manuscript development, in the UCSF Department of Orthopaedic Surgery. Suited to someone planning a path into orthopaedics, digital health, or surgical innovation.

level1_desc: You take on a defined part of a study with close mentorship, and learn data handling, the literature, and how a question becomes a result. Sustained contributors earn authorship.
level2_desc: You run your own clinical or computational project from question to submission, and present at AAOS and allied meetings, building a focused body of published work during medical school.
level3_desc: Bring a clinical question and use the lab's existing data, tooling, and mentorship to pursue it without building infrastructure from scratch. Work spans biomechanics, robotics, and large-database studies.
level4_desc: If you work in machine learning, biomechanics, causal inference, or related fields, we have clinical problems and the data to pursue them. We host visiting scholars and maintain collaborations across institutions.
values_text: We care most about initiative. People do well here when they bring their own ideas and carry them through to publication. The technical skills (programming, statistics, imaging, mechanics) can be learned in the lab.
apply_text: Read through the research and find what you would want to work on, then send the short application form. If there is a fit, we will talk it through and settle on the right project.
